title: LEGAL SPECIALIST AFFARI REGOLATORI
description:
Il Gruppo Unoenergy è un punto di riferimento nel settore energetico, attivo dal 2003 nel mercato libero del gas naturale, dell'energia elettrica e dei servizi di efficientamento energetico.

  

Con una presenza consolidata in Italia attraverso uffici commerciali, punti vendita e una rete di consulenti energetici altamente qualificati, l’impegno dell’azienda non è solo nella fornitura di servizi, ma anche nell'offrire soluzioni innovative per promuovere il risparmio energetico e un suo utilizzo razionale.

  

Da oltre due decenni, il Gruppo Unoenergy si distingue come uno dei pionieri nell'ambito della vendita di gas naturale ed energia elettrica, garantendo un approccio basato sull'affidabilità, sulla passione e sulla professionalità.

  

Unoenergy opera con capitale interamente privato e serve oggi una vasta gamma di clienti: residenziali, business e condomini.

  

Per il Gruppo lo sviluppo delle persone è fondamentale per il successo aziendale. Pertanto, la politica di gestione delle Risorse Umane si basa su principi chiave come la creazione di occupazione stabile e di qualità, l'attrazione e la fidelizzazione dei talenti, la promozione di una comunicazione interna efficace, il rispetto delle pari opportunità e l'eliminazione di qualsiasi forma di discriminazione. Il Gruppo è orgoglioso di identificarsi con un rigoroso codice etico che guida le azioni aziendali quotidiane.

  

Scopri di più su unoenergy.it

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

La **Direzione Affari Legali** del Gruppo Unoenergy cerca per il proprio ufficio di Milano un/a Legal Specialist Affari Regolatori che si occuperà di:

* Supportare il monitoraggio normativo regolatorio gas \& power
* Elaborare una sintesi delle disposizioni regolatorie di interesse per l’azienda
* Analizzare gli impatti regolatori sulle attività aziendali
* Verificare la compliance delle procedure aziendali all’evoluzione del quadro regolatorio

**Requisiti:**
--------------

* Laurea in discipline giuridiche
* Pregressa esperienza nell’attività di almeno 3 anni
* Familiarità con disposizioni dell’Autorità di Regolazione per Energia Reti e Ambienti (ARERA) e conoscenza della regolazione primaria e secondaria disciplinante il settore dell’energia elettrica e del gas naturale.
* Ottime doti relazionali e capacità di lavorare in team
* Capacità di sintesi e proattività.
* Spiccata attitudine al problem solving

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

**Offriamo:**

* Assunzione stabile con inquadramento commisurato all'esperienza e potenziale
* Formazione e affiancamento continui
* Smart working fino a 2 gg alla settimana
* Inserimento in contesto aziendale solido, dinamico ed in continua crescita
* CCNL Commercio
* 14 mensilità
* Benefits aziendali

 **Sede di lavoro**: Milano (MI).

title: ICT Security Manager
description:
Vibe Srl \- società di consulenza informatica e sviluppo software specializzata nel settore ICT, è alla ricerca di professionisti qualificati per l'inserimento immediato nel proprio organico:

**ICT Security Manager**

Siamo alla ricerca di un\* ICT Security Manager di comprovata esperienza che assuma la responsabilità della protezione del patrimonio informativo e della definizione delle strategie di cybersecurity.

**Missione:**

* Definisce la politica di sicurezza del sistema informativo;
* Gestisce l’implementazione della sicurezza del sistema informatico;
* Assicura la disponibilità delle informazioni;
* È riconosciuto come esperto di politiche di sicurezza ICT da soggetti interni e esterni.

**Attività principali:**

* Definisce e implementa le procedure legate alla sicurezza ICT;
* Contribuisce allo sviluppo della politica di sicurezza dell’Amministrazione;
* Stabilisce il piano di prevenzione;
* Informa e sensibilizza l’Amministrazione sulla politica di sicurezza;
* Garantisce la promozione delle regole di sicurezza ICT tra gli utenti;
* Controlla e garantisce l’applicazione dei principi e delle regole per la sicurezza dell’ICT.

**Requisiti Tecnici e Professionali**:

* **Esperienza:** Almeno 10 anni di esperienza consolidata in ambito ICT, con focus specifico sulla Cybersecurity.
* **Competenze Core:** Conoscenza approfondita delle architetture di rete, protocolli di sicurezza, crittografia, sistemi di rilevamento intrusioni (IDS/IPS) e gestione delle vulnerabilità.
* **Certificazioni Obbligatorie:** Possesso di certificazioni in ambito sicurezza.
* **Soft Skills:** Eccellenti doti comunicative, autorevolezza tecnica e capacità di mediazione tra esigenze di business e requisiti di sicurezza.

*Modalità e sede di lavoro: Remote*

Invitiamo i candidati interessati a inviare il proprio curriculum vitae in formato Word, dettagliando accuratamente i ruoli ricoperti e le tecnologie utilizzate, completo di autorizzazione al trattamento dei dati personali.

Il presente annuncio è rivolto a entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno, Tempo indeterminato

Retribuzione: €40\.000,00 \- €45\.000,00 all'anno

Domande di preselezione:

* Quanti anni di esperienza complessiva ha maturato nel settore ICT?
* Quali certificazioni possiede attualmente in ambito Cybersecurity?
* Ha esperienza diretta nella gestione di piani di prevenzione e disaster recovery in ambienti full cloud o ibridi?
* Dobbiamo coprire questa posizione con urgenza. Potresti iniziare subito?

title: Ingegnere/Tecnico per Gestione Obsolescenza Materiali - ferrotranviario
description:
***Chi Siamo:***


Axcent Company è un gruppo di società di System Integration, fondato nel 2008 e presente in Italia, con uffici a Milano e a Napoli. La società fornisce servizi in ambito Engineering, Information Technology, Capital Markets e Treasury Management. Siamo coinvolti in grandi progetti, preparati e reattivi ad ogni esigenza, le nostre competenze e la nostra formazione professionale rendono possibile assistere al meglio il business di chi ci sceglie.


Puoi trovare ulteriori dettagli sul nostro sito web *axcentcompany.it*

 ***Chi stiamo cercando***


Con l’obiettivo di rafforzare il nostro team, siamo alla ricerca di **Ingegneri per la gestione dell’obsolescenza dei materiali** da inserire all’interno di progetti particolarmente strutturati nel settore ferrotranviario. Cerchiamo una figura tecnica altamente qualificata per supportare le attività di manutenzione dei mezzi su rotaia (tram e metropolitane) attraverso l’analisi dei materiali e la gestione dell’obsolescenza dei componenti. Il candidato ideale sarà responsabile dell’identificazione di materiali critici, della valutazione della loro disponibilità sul mercato e della proposta di soluzioni alternative in caso di componenti non più reperibili.

 ***Mission***


L’obiettivo è gestire le seguenti fasi lavorative:


Stesura documenti tecnici (capitolati di gara, disegni, specifiche, relazioni);


Test a bordo treno/tram;


Analisi e risoluzione delle problematiche tecniche relative ai rotabili metropolitani e tranviari;


Monitorare lo stato di obsolescenza dei materiali e proporre soluzioni tecniche (retrofit, reverse engineering, fornitori alternativi);


Collaborare con il team di manutenzione, ingegneria e acquisti per garantire la continuità operativa;


Ricerca alternativa dei prodotti per obsolescenza o per economia;


Supporto al contenzioso tecnico con i fornitori;


Analisi critica della documentazione tecnica a corredo dei rotabili (Manuali, Elaborati di Progetto, ecc…).

 ***Esperienza richiesta***


Si valutano candidature con esperienza in attività analoghe. In particolare:


Esperienza 15 anni nell’ambito dell’ufficio tecnico


Esperienza 9 anni nelle attività di reverse\-engineering in ambito metroferrotranviario


Esperienza 9 anni per attività di monitoraggio delle obsolescenze in ambito elettronico

 ***Must have***


Laurea Magistrale in Ingegneria Meccanica/Elettrica/Gestionale o equivalente.


È gradita una buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.

 ***Technical Skills***


Conoscenze elettroniche


Capacità di lettura e interpretazione di disegni tecnici e distinte base.


Uso di strumenti ERP

 ***Soft skills***


La risorsa dovrà essere particolarmente proattiva ed avere ottime doti relazionali e comunicative con capacità di problem solving, buone capacità di analisi e risoluzione dei problemi, proattività ed atteggiamento positivo, precisione, puntualità ed accuratezza.

 ***Cosa ti offriamo***


Sede di lavoro: Milano


Orario di lavoro: full time


Disponibilità: Da concordare

 ***Il nostro gruppo:***

**Axcent System Engineering** è l’area specializzata nella consulenza sui settori Mobility e Smart Cities con oltre 15 anni di esperienza. Al centro dei nostri progetti c’è la qualità che garantiamo offrendo supporto e consulenza di alto livello. Siamo dei System Integrator sempre alla ricerca delle migliori soluzioni per offrire innovazione ai nostri Clienti.

 **Axcent Technology Solutions** è l’area che ha maturato una vasta esperienza nell'ingegneria, nello sviluppo, nel testing e nell'implementazione del software, nei settori Telco, Finance, Public Administration e Industry \& Energy. Forniamo consulenza e servizi tecnologicamente avanzati, aiutando i partner a raggiungere i propri Obiettivi di Business, attraverso la produzione di soluzioni tailor made.


Dall'esperienza maturata nel settore dell'ingegneria del software nasce la Software Factory di Axcent, un vero e proprio Key Skills Hub. La nostra Factory è un centro di produzione in cui il team opera in stretta sinergia, seguendo il prodotto progettato dallo sviluppo alla sua manutenzione.

 **WMCM Advisory** è l’area multidisciplinare in cui aiutiamo i nostri clienti a creare valore, massimizzare la crescita e migliorare la performance aziendale. Grazie alle competenze ed esperienze dei nostri team di professionisti altamente specializzati, operiamo in ambito Capital Market \& Asset Management e Banking.

 **Axcent Company** è impegnata a promuovere un ambiente di lavoro diversificato e inclusivo. Tutte le candidature saranno valutate senza considerare età, genere, orientamento sessuale, etnia o disabilità.


Unisciti a noi e contribuisci al successo continuo di Axcent Company, dove la qualità e l'innovazione sono al centro di ogni progetto.

 *Inviare Curriculum Vitae corredato della seguente dicitura: “Con la presente manifesto il mio assenso attivo al trattamento dei miei dati personali ai sensi del GDPR 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation)”. L’autorizzazione dovrà essere sottoscritta con firma olografa dell’interessato. Qualora non ricevessimo tale autorizzazione, non potremo procedere all’elaborazione completa del Curriculum.*

 *Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03\.*

title: ERP D365 F&O - Finance - Junior Consulente Funzionale
description:

description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**DESCRIPTION**


Join the PPI Digital Transformation organization at Thermo Fisher Scientific as a **Digital Project Manager**, where you will be responsible for the end\-to\-end delivery of digital initiatives across manufacturing sites.


In this role, you will translate business and operational needs into executable digital projects, coordinating cross\-functional teams that include Data Engineering, Data Science, IIoT, IT, Automation, and site stakeholders.


You will own execution, planning, risk management, and change management for assigned initiatives, ensuring that solutions are delivered safely, compliantly, and with verified business impact.


The position operates in a **multi\-site, matrix environment** and works in close collaboration with technical domain leads and senior technical experts, while reporting directly to the PPI Digital Transformation leadership.


Strong stakeholder management, execution discipline, and focus on value realization are critical for success.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Digital Project Delivery**

* Manage the full lifecycle of assigned digital initiatives, from opportunity framing through deployment and value realization.
* Translate business needs into clear project charters, scope, milestones, resource plans, and success criteria, using PPI and Lean principles.
* Build and maintain detailed project plans, RAID logs, and status reporting; proactively identify risks and define mitigation actions.
* Coordinate cross\-functional teams and external partners to ensure on\-time, on\-budget, and in\-scope delivery.

**Digital Roadmap \& Portfolio Support**

* Support the prioritization and sequencing of digital use cases in alignment with site strategy, PPI targets, and divisional roadmaps.
* Provide visibility on capacity, dependencies, and constraints across parallel initiatives to enable realistic planning.
* Contribute to annual and multi\-year digital planning activities.

**Business Case \& Benefits Realization**

* Develop robust business cases for digital initiatives in collaboration with site functions.
* Track benefits using a bottom\-up approach aligned with site PPI savings plans.
* Define and monitor KPIs for each initiative; ensure benefits are validated with Finance and business owners.
* Provide accurate input to digital and PPI performance reviews and dashboards.

**Stakeholder Engagement \& Change Management**

* Act as the primary point of contact for assigned digital initiatives at site level.
* Lead change management activities, including stakeholder mapping, communication plans, training coordination, and adoption tracking.
* Facilitate workshops, Kaizens, and design sessions to define current state, future state, and implementation roadmaps.
* Ensure digital solutions are embedded into standard work and PPI routines, avoiding isolated pilots without sustained adoption.

**Governance, Compliance \& Standards**

* Apply divisional PPI \& Digital governance models, standards, and templates consistently.
* Ensure alignment with IT/OT architecture, cybersecurity, data standards, and validation requirements.
* Coordinate with IT, OT, and Data \& Analytics teams to ensure data quality and compliance with GxP, data integrity, and privacy requirements.

**Site \& Collaboration Model**


The role supports digital initiatives across multiple manufacturing sites, operating in a **distributed, cross\-site model** and collaborating with stakeholders across EU and APAC locations. Occasional travel to sites may be required.

**REQUIREMENTS**

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Business, or related field.
* **5\+ years of experience** in project management, continuous improvement, operations, or digital transformation roles within manufacturing or regulated environments.
* Proven experience delivering digital, automation, data/analytics, or manufacturing IT initiatives.
* Strong project management skills, including scope definition, planning, stakeholder management, and risk control.
* Solid understanding of GMP manufacturing and quality processes.
* Fluency in English and Italian.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Master’s degree in a relevant field.
* Experience in pharmaceutical, biotech, CDMO, or other highly regulated industries.
* Familiarity with MES, LIMS, historians, data platforms, and BI tools.
* Experience with Agile or hybrid delivery approaches.
* Lean / Six Sigma / PPI certification (Green Belt or equivalent).
* Formal project management certification (PMP, PRINCE2\).
* Change management certification (e.g. PROSCI).

**Key Competencies \& Soft Skills**

* Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across all organizational levels.
* Ability to work effectively in a **matrix and cross\-functional environment**.
* Structured, analytical, and results\-oriented mindset.
* Ability to balance execution discipline with flexibility and “test \& learn” approaches.
* High learning agility and curiosity toward digital technologies and new ways of working.
* Strong commitment to safety, quality, compliance, and company values.

**Additional Information**

* Travel may be required (**up to 30%**).

description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Able to lift 40 lbs. without assistance**Job Description**

**We are taking open applications for Field Service Engineer roles within the Electron Microscope field!**


At this time, we are open to applications for upcoming roles expected in February/March across Italy, France \& Germany. We are gathering interest for future opportunities across these locations and more broadly across Europe. If you express interest, we will review your application and the Talent Acquisition team may contact you when the role becomes available or sooner. Please note that it might be several weeks until someone contacts you and the first contact is usually via email.

**Field Service Engineer II**

When you join us at Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll be part of an inquisitive team that shares your passion for exploration and discovery. With revenues of more than $40 billion and the largest investment in R\&D in the industry, we give our people the resources and chances to create significant contributions to the world.

**Division Specific Information**


The Materials and Structural Analysis (MSD) Electron Microscopy (EM) business enables customers to find meaningful answers to questions that accelerate breakthrough discoveries and ultimately change the world. The MSD Electron Microscopy (EM) business designs, manufactures, and supports the broadest range of high\-performance microscopy workflow solutions that provide images and answers in the micro\-, nano\-, and picometer scales.

**How You Will Make an Impact**: Field Service Engineers provide technical support and assistance, ensuring the efficient performance of equipment and systems. You will work directly with customers to address their needs, ensuring customer satisfaction and product efficiency.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Manage and complete more complex service tasks independently.
* Ensure compliance with safety and regulatory standards.
* Ensure accurate and detailed service reports are generated.
* Provide expertise and recommendations on technical issues.
* Collaborate with clients to understand their needs and tailor solutions.
* Offer technical training support to Field Service Engineer I.
* Participate in work schedules creation with colleagues and/or management.
* Provide sales leads and feedback to support teams.

**Education**

* Bachelor’s degree or higher in mechatronics, electronics/engineering, or equivalent.

**Experience**

* 2\-5 years of experience in a related field.
* + University degree with some experience of hands\-on technical experience installing, troubleshooting and repairing complex analytical instruments, ideally of Electron\-Microscopes or similar technology.

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities**

* Analytical troubleshooting and problem\-solving abilities.
* Interest in technology and IT trends.
* Knowledge of electron microscopes is an advantage.

**Physical Requirements / Work Environment**

Excellent hand\-eye coordination and manual dexterity.

**Benefits**


We offer competitive remuneration, annual incentive plan bonus, healthcare, and a range of employee benefits. Thermo Fisher Scientific offers employment with an innovative, forward\-thinking organization, and outstanding career and development prospects. We offer an exciting company culture that stands for integrity, intensity, involvement, and innovation!

title: Corporate Coverage Analyst - GCIB
description:
**Do you want your voice heard and your actions to count?**

Discover your opportunity with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s leading financial groups. Across the globe, we’re 150,000 colleagues, striving to make a difference for every client, organization, and community we serve. We stand for our values, building long\-term relationships, serving society, and fostering shared and sustainable growth for a better world.


With a vision to be the world’s most trusted financial group, it’s part of our culture to put people first, listen to new and diverse ideas and collaborate toward greater innovation, speed and agility. This means investing in talent, technologies, and tools that empower you to own your career.


Join MUFG, where being inspired is expected and making a meaningful impact is rewarded.

**About us:**


GCIB Italy manages MUFG’s relationships with leading Italian corporates, financial institutions, financial sponsors, Italian subsidiaries of multinational groups and Italian companies owned by private equity or infrastructure funds.


Leveraging MUFG’s global network and comprehensive product platform, together with our product specialists we support clients across corporate finance, capital markets, structured solutions, leveraged and project finance and strategic advisory needs.

**Role Overview:**


We are seeking a highly motivated Analyst to join our Global Corporate \& Investment Banking team in Milan. This role represents the first step in a long\-term career path toward senior client coverage or product origination positions.


The Analyst will be fully integrated into the team from day one, working closely with Senior Bankers and Product Specialists and contributing directly to client activities, deal execution, and strategic initiatives.

**Key Responsibilities:**


1\. Client Coverage \& Business Origination

* Support Senior Bankers in daily client interaction, contributing to idea generation and origination across a wide range of products.
* Assist in identifying new business opportunities and support onboarding of prospective clients.
* Prepare high\-quality marketing materials, pitch books, and presentations for client meetings.
* Contribute to the development and execution of Account Plans, ensuring cross\-sell opportunities are maximized.
* Draft clear and accurate call reports to maintain strong internal documentation and follow\-up.


2\. Deal Assessment \& Execution

* Develop a solid understanding of credit fundamentals across corporate finance, leveraged finance, project finance and fund finance.
* Prepare Deal Screening Memos, business cases and internal submissions in collaboration with Senior Bankers and Product Partners.
* Act as first point of contact for internal procedures, supporting execution processes including documentation coordination, checklists, compliance requirements and KYC.


3\. Risk, Governance \& Internal Reporting

* Apply MUFG’s risk culture and support the risk assessment framework through thoughtful analysis and disciplined documentation.
* Gain ownership of KYC, onboarding processes and internal reporting requirements.
* Ensure full compliance with internal standards, governance requirements and regulatory expectations.

**What We Are Looking For**


Education \& Experience

* Final\-year students or recent graduates (up to 12 months) are encouraged to apply.
* Candidates with up to 2 years of experience in a top\-tier financial institution are welcome; experience in Debt Advisory, Leveraged Finance, Project Finance, or related fields is an advantage but not essential.
* Internal applicants with the right motivation and skills will also be considered.


Skills \& Competencies

* Strong motivation to work in a client\-facing, high\-performance environment.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across multiple teams.
* Proactive mindset, intellectual curiosity and strong commitment to continuous learning.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment and deliver high\-quality output under time pressure.
* Team\-player mentality combined with the ability to develop independent insights and propose thoughtful solutions.
* High proficiency with Microsoft Office tools and strong analytical capabilities.
* Fluency in Italian and English is required; additional European languages or Japanese are a plus.


Culture \& Values


Client Centric

* Always ask the question “how does this impact our Clients?”
* Think like our Clients and advocate for their success.
* Listen to the voice of the Client to drive innovation.
* Be a trusted advisor by providing thoughtful, integrated solutions to meet our Client’s unique needs.


People Focused

* Connect beyond your Team and leverage our global strength as One MUFG.
* Value contributions and results over titles and hierarchy.
* Respect each other, assume good intent, and listen reflectively.
* Develop yourself and others, learn from mistakes and recognise achievements.


Listen Up. Speak Up

* Actively listen to diverse perspectives before voicing opinions or making decisions.
* Ask the difficult questions to stretch our thinking and expand our mind\-set.
* Call out behaviours that don’t align with our values, conduct and culture.
* Propose innovative ideas even when they may be unpopular.


Innovate \& Simplify

* Be resilient and challenge the status quo to support transformation and innovation.
* Simplify what does not need to be complex.
* Be curious about what’s next and doing it better.
* Expect efficiency and manage Company resources as if they were your own.


Own \& Execute

* Execute with speed, simplicity and transparency to manage risk and drive results.
* Empower those closest to the issue to solve it.
* Be accountable for your actions and accept responsibility.
* Pro\-actively prioritize competing objectives to deliver high performance.

**What We Offer**


Joining GCIB Italy means becoming part of a high\-performing, globally connected platform where talent is recognised, supported and accelerated. We offer:

* A steep learning curve and meaningful responsibility from day one, with direct exposure to senior bankers, product experts and top\-tier clients.
* A structured development path, including technical and soft\-skill training, mentorship, on\-the\-job learning and access to MUFG’s global knowledge network.
* Broad client and product exposure, across corporate lending, leveraged finance, project finance, structured solutions and international coverage teams.
* A collaborative and inclusive culture, where individual contribution is valued and teamwork is at the core of success.
* Career growth opportunities, with a long\-term development track designed to progress toward Associate and Senior roles within Client Coverage or Product Origination.
* International perspective, through interaction with MUFG colleagues across EMEA, the Americas and Asia.
* Competitive compensation and benefits, aligned with the standards of leading global financial institutions.
* A modern hybrid working model (1 day per week remote), designed to support productivity, personal development and team cohesion.


We are open to considering flexible working requests in line with organisational requirements.


MUFG is committed to embracing diversity and building an inclusive culture where all employees are valued, respected and their opinions count. We support the principles of equality, diversity and inclusion in recruitment and employment, and oppose all forms of discrimination on the grounds of age, sex, gender, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy and maternity, race, gender reassignment, religion or belief and marriage or civil partnership.


We make our recruitment decisions in a non\-discriminatory manner in accordance with our commitment to identifying the right skills for the right role and our obligations under the law.

**KPMG Advisory S.p.A. garantisce le pari opportunità.**

In particular, by joining the **Enterprise Platforms Team**, you will be involved in **SAP FI (Financial Accounting)** implementation projects for **Public Sector clients**, contributing to requirements analysis and to defining solutions that support administrative and accounting processes and period\-end closing activities.

title: Risk Analyst (Italian Speaking)
description:
IT Milan


\|
Risk Monitoring


\|
7 Apr 2026


\|
R\-0004613


  
**We’re reinventing payments.**  

In less than four years, Dojo disrupted the market to become the largest and most loved acquirer in the UK. Our payments infrastructure, purpose\-built for in\-person commerce, is game changing.



Now, over 150,000 customers across four countries choose to transact billions with us every year.  

But we’re just getting started.



Our people are the driving force behind our success. They are our greatest investment and our ultimate competitive advantage. We hire exceptional people and give them the autonomy, trust, and ownership to thrive. The results take care of themselves.

### **The Role**



We are looking for a detail\-oriented and proactive Italian\-speaking Fraud \& Risk Analyst to join our Risk team. This role will be based in our Milan office. This isn't just a data\-entry role; you will be on the front lines, investigating suspicious activity, protecting our merchants from financial loss, and helping us scale our risk framework. You will be a key player in maintaining the integrity of our platform across our Italian market, working closely with Underwriting and AML along with other teams within risk.


### **What You Will Do**


* Investigative Analysis: Review and investigate real\-time suspicious transaction alerts triggered by our fraud monitoring systems.
* Merchant Monitoring: Regularly audit merchant activity reports to identify potential fraud patterns, credit risks, or financial irregularities before they escalate.
* Merchant Advocacy \& Education (Italian Market): Act as a supportive partner to our Italian\-speaking merchants. You will communicate directly with them in fluent Italian to provide education on best practices, helping them minimize their exposure to fraud and chargebacks.
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Partner with the Underwriting and AML teams to share insights on emerging trends, ensuring our internal policies and risk appetites remain current and effective.
* Operational Excellence: Support senior team members with ad\-hoc projects and strategic initiatives. You’ll also play a vital role in maintaining our risk documentation to ensure our processes remain robust as we scale.
* Team Contribution: Work within a high\-trust, collaborative environment where supporting your peers is just as important as managing your own queue.

  





### **What You Will Bring**


* Language Fluency: Professional fluency in Italian (written and verbal) is a core requirement for this role. You must be comfortable conducting difficult conversations and explaining complex risk concepts to native speakers.
* Relevant Experience: A solid background in Risk Management, Fraud Investigation, or AML. You understand the red flags that signal suspicious behavior.
* Industry Knowledge: Ideally, you have experience within the payments or card processing industry. If you know your way around chargebacks, schemes, and merchant acquiring, you’ll hit the ground running.
* Growth Mindset: A genuine passion for the world of FinTech and a hunger to upskill in fraud prevention techniques and regulatory compliance.
* Communication Skills: A high standard of written and verbal communication in both English and Italian. You can translate complex risk issues into clear, actionable advice.
* Adaptability: You thrive in a fast\-paced environment where priorities can shift. You are comfortable making quick, informed decisions without sacrificing accuracy.
* Technical Literacy: Comfort using fraud monitoring tools and a general proficiency in documenting processes clearly and concisely.

**Dojo home and away**



We believe our best work happens when we collaborate in\-person. These “together days” foster communication, drive innovation and spark our brightest ideas.



That's why we have an office\-first culture. This means working from the office 4\+ days per week.



With offices across Europe, we know a thing or two about staying dynamic. Need deep focus? Head to a quiet zone. Big ideas? Collaboration spaces have you covered. Just here for a catch\-up? Our social hubs make it easy. Do work that counts, in spaces made for you.

  

**Question: what’s curious, relentless, and customer obsessed?**



If you’re keen to know the answer, you’re a third of the way to meeting our Dojo values.



If the following speak to you, let’s talk:


* You’re curious. You have a real desire to learn and create.
* You’re relentless. You keep going even when it’s easier not to.
* You’re customer\-obsessed. You know how important customers are to what you do.

  


#### **Diversity, equity, and inclusion at Dojo**



From local bakeries to well\-known eateries, Dojo payments serve over 150,000 places across the UK.



And something that’s fundamental to creating relevant, innovative products at Dojo is to build teams to reflect the diversity of the businesses we serve.



Our drive to improve diversity, equity, and inclusion is closely linked to helping employees thrive and innovating for better customer experiences.



If you care about your work, you’re curious, and you think customer\-first, you have a place at Dojo.



To make sure you’re the best you can be throughout the recruitment process, let us know if you need any extra adjustments to help you thrive.

  



Visit dojo.careers to find out more about our benefits and what it’s like to work at Dojo, or check out our LinkedIn and Instagram pages.



\#LI\-Hybrid

title: Venture Capital Analyst
description:
**Sella Direct Ventures** is the venture capital arm of **Sella Group**, one of the largest private and independent banking group in Italy. We have over 5,000 employees, we are present in 7 countries, and we have 300 branches throughout Italy. Our activities include commercial and retail banking, private and wealth management, corporate \& investment banking, payment systems, open banking, and information technology services.

**Sella Direct** **Ventures** operates with a single\-LP structure — with Sella Group as our sole investor — combining the discipline and deal\-making approach of an independent VC with the strategic resources, network, and domain expertise of a major financial institution.


We back early\-stage startups building the future of financial services, covering the full fintech spectrum, from embedded finance to payments and financial infrastructure. We operate as a lean, high\-conviction fund with a European focus and a global mindset. We can act as a strategic partner — not just a capital provider — for the founders we back.


We are looking for a driven and intellectually curious **Analyst** to join our team.

**The Role**


As an Analyst at Sella Direct Ventures, you will be a core part of a small, ambitious team where your work has real impact from day one. You will be involved across the full investment lifecycle — from identifying and evaluating new opportunities to supporting portfolio companies as they scale.


This is a generalist role with a strong bias toward analytical rigor, intellectual honesty, and genuine curiosity about where financial services are headed.

**What You'll Do**

**Deal Sourcing \& Evaluation**

* Proactively identify, map, and track high\-potential startups across our investment themes
* Build and maintain relationships with founders, co\-investors, and the broader European VC ecosystem
* Attend industry events, demos, and conferences to strengthen deal flow and market knowledge

**Investment Process**

* Lead and support due diligence across all dimensions: market sizing, competitive landscape, business model analysis, unit economics, and team assessment
* Build financial models to evaluate deal structures and returns
* Prepare investment memos and present recommendations to the Investment Committee

**Market \& Thematic Research**

* Develop and maintain investment theses across key verticals and emerging trends
* Monitor regulatory developments, market dynamics, and technology shifts relevant to our focus areas
* Produce internal research reports and sector deep\-dives

**Portfolio Support**

* Assist portfolio companies with strategic introductions, hiring, and access to the Sella network
* Support reporting and monitoring of portfolio performance

**What We're Looking For**

**Experience**

* 1–3 years in venture capital, growth equity, investment banking, strategy consulting, or a high\-growth startup/scaleup
* Direct exposure to financial services or fintech is a strong plus, though not a strict requirement

**Skills**

* Strong analytical and structured thinking — you can break down complex problems and communicate them clearly
* Financial modelling proficiency and comfort with both quantitative and qualitative analysis
* Excellent written and verbal communication in both **English** and **Italian**
* High proficiency with MS Office / Google Workspace and familiarity with AI tools

**Mindset**

* Genuine passion for technology, startups, and the future of financial services
* Intellectually curious — you read broadly, form opinions, and enjoy being challenged
* Proactive and autonomous — you take ownership without waiting to be told what to do
* A natural relationship\-builder who engages authentically with founders, investors, and operators
* Comfortable in a lean, fast\-moving environment where priorities shift and ambiguity is the norm

**What We Offer**

* A front\-row seat to the European fintech and venture ecosystem
* Exposure to the full investment cycle within an active, well\-networked team
* Direct access to and mentorship from senior leaders across Sella Group
* A genuine voice in investment decisions from early on
* The opportunity to build your network across founders, co\-investors, and operators across Europe
* A collaborative and intellectually stimulating environment
* Apprenticeship contract with structured learning and on\-the\-job development, in an innovative and inclusive culture that values ideas and continuous improvement.
* Flexible home office (i.e., smart working) up to 13 days/month on average and additional paid leave to support work–life balance.
* Comprehensive total rewards package including meal vouchers, performance\-related bonus, flexible welfare, supplementary pension scheme, and preferential banking \& insurance conditions.
* Strong focus on wellbeing, with private health and life insurance, mental and physical wellbeing services, and access to a benefits platform with discounts and offers.

*Sella Direct Ventures \& the Sella Group are committed to building an inclusive and sustainable work environment. We believe diverse perspectives make us better investors and partners, and we actively welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds.*
